name: Code Generation Agents
description: Autonomous agents that write, execute, debug, and iterate on code to complete programming tasks. The feedback loop — write code, run it, observe error, revise — enables self-correction that purely generative LLMs cannot do. Coding agents are the highest-fidelity test of general agent capability because code has objective correctness criteria.
contrarian_take: SWE-bench pass rates are the current proxy for coding agent quality, but the benchmark rewards narrow patch generation over deep architectural understanding. High SWE-bench scores do not generalize to novel codebases, refactoring tasks, or system design. Papers that evaluate agents on real-world repository complexity — not curated issues — tell a different and more sobering story.
example_signals: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
Devin: The First AI Software Engineer
SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
